summary: Strong proposal: all 3 novel_dim conditions parse cleanly via supported fields, structurally distinct from existing OI_Price entries, and triggers estimate lands in the adequate-to-tight range with broad exit compatibility skewed toward fixed.

analyses.distinctness_notes: E126/E064 are the winning taker_buy templates but on pricechange channel; E051 uses high_liquidity on pricechange. No existing OI_Price entry combines taker_buy_ratio_5m + liquidity_bucket=high + oi_change_pct threshold as a triple-AND. The 61 OI_Price archetypes appear to focus on OI/funding/global_ratio dimensions, not taker-flow + liquidity. Cross-channel port of a proven pricechange template to OI_Price is structurally novel and tests whether the taker-buy edge generalizes beyond pricechange.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 1400 OI_Price events × 0.30 (oi_change_pct>=10 is moderately common on OI_Price by construction) × 0.50 (high_liquidity bucket ~top tercile) × 0.50 (taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 is roughly p70-p75 in pump regimes) ≈ 105
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Coverage table shows fixed dominates (mean +0.175, median +0.217, 6680/7320 positive) while multi_tp/trail/time_only/breakeven all have median<0 and zero positives — strongly recommend prioritizing fixed exits. Given E126's R2 paper-trade trap (TP=0.51/SL=6.00 asymmetry compounded to -13.5%), pair with symmetric or capped-asymmetry fixed grids and validate on mean/kelly_capped/p25_capped_tail rather than legacy p25. Breakeven could be a secondary screen to neutralize left-tail risk on this template.
